reason: Initialized the first .cockpit runtime scaffold from repo specs and examples.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the bounded runbook directive.
Advanced from the completed runbook task to the first live PCC control-loop proof task.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the first live control-loop proof directive.
Advanced from the completed proof task to the first actual product-work task focused on deterministic state advancement.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the deterministic state-advance helper directive.
Resolved PASS-completion semantics and advanced to the next bounded product-work task focused on deterministic worker-directive generation.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the deterministic worker-directive generation task.
Advanced to the next bounded product-work task to remove the remaining hardcoded Current Truth facts from the directive generator by moving them into canonical repo truth.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the canonical Current Truth sourcing task.
Advanced to a bounded restart-safety task focused on generating a fresh-advisor restart brief from canonical truth.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the fresh-advisor restart brief task.
Advanced to the worker-side restart-safety proof so a fresh Claude session can resume from canonical repo truth without owner re-briefing.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the fresh worker-session restart-safety proof task.
Advanced to the final natural-break task: a bounded dual-restart drill covering both fresh advisor and fresh worker sessions.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the dual-restart proof cycle and confirmed both fresh session restart paths work from canonical repo truth.
Advanced from the natural break to a bounded enforcement follow-on that makes restart-safety validation a required gate before fresh-session handoff artifacts are treated as ready.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the handoff enforcement task and confirmed the new gate blocks stale handoffs while passing valid restart-safe fresh-session packets from canonical repo truth.
Promoted the top-ranked backup idea into a bounded task focused on passive protected-file backup and restore so risky PCC cycles have a cheap local recovery path.
Reviewed Claude worker output against the protected-file backup task and confirmed the helper creates restore points, restores chosen snapshots, and stays passive and non-gating.
Promoted the next ranked idea into a bounded task for a read-only advisory doctor command that summarizes repo health without becoming a gate.

Reviewed Claude worker output against the advisory doctor task; independently re-ran doctor.ps1 and validate-cockpit-state.ps1 and confirmed it composes existing checks, reports OK/WARN/ISSUE, and always exits 0 without gating anything.
Promoted the next ranked idea into a bounded task for a read-only safe-stop command that confirms resumability before ending a session, without advancing state or gating.

Reviewed Claude worker output against the safe-stop task; independently re-ran the checks against a fresh scratch copy and confirmed via checksums the command writes nothing to state or handoff files.

Secondary independent verifier (ChatGPT) flagged that next_action and last_verified_handoff still described pcc-v1-012 close-out as pending after it had completed. Confirmed a real propagation gap in advance-cockpit-state.ps1, corrected both fields, and recorded the root cause as backlog IDEA-011 rather than silently re-patching it each cycle.

Promoted the next ranked idea into a bounded task for a deterministic append-only activity-log helper, motivated directly by this session's own manual routing-log.jsonl backfill (several real entries were missed and had to be hand-corrected).

detail: Independently re-ran log-event.ps1's key claims from a fresh scratch copy: confirmed only Add-Content is used to write (structural append-only guarantee), re-tested the invalid-EventType guard with a correctly-checked exit code (the worker's own first test attempt had a shell-pipe masking issue, which they caught themselves), tested derived mode against a different archived verification result than the worker used and confirmed correct task_id/event_type/detail derivation, and independently confirmed via diff that prior log lines were untouched. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
Promoted IDEA-011 into a bounded task to fix advance-cockpit-state.ps1's stale next_action and last_verified_handoff after close-out, root-caused during pcc-v1-012/pcc-v1-013.

Independently reproduced the core fix from a fresh scratch copy using a different synthetic task_id and a different deliberately-planted stale-checklist next_action string, confirming it does not leak through for a PASS verdict and that the archived path is used correctly. Also independently tested the BLOCKED verdict path (not covered by the worker's own tests) and confirmed non-PASS behavior is unaffected. Confirmed via diff that docs/DECISIONS.md was correctly left untouched, matching allowed scope. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.

Promoted IDEA-003 (last open V1 backlog item) into a bounded task after a spike confirmed pwsh's Test-Json correctly handles our schema features. Owner directed: light, non-blocking, folded into doctor.ps1.

Independently re-ran doctor.ps1 against the live repo (confirmed OK) and reproduced the ISSUE path in a fresh scratch copy using a missing-required-field violation, different from either corruption the worker tested, confirming the check genuinely generalizes rather than being tuned to the worker's specific test cases. Confirmed via grep that check-schemas.ps1 is referenced only by doctor.ps1, never wired as a gate. This closes out IDEA-003, the last open V1 backlog item. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
Drafted first BRR Phase 1 policy task (Owner Review Matrix) and refreshed worker/advisor handoff artifacts.

Reviewed the new BRR policy doc and its propagated truth-surface updates against the task boundaries and completion criteria. The repo now has a concrete canonical Owner Review Matrix in docs/BRR_POLICY.md, all 11 required owner-review cases are covered, the owner_decisions ambiguity is resolved as a small STATE_MODEL clarification, and no runtime or out-of-scope changes were introduced. All completion criteria met.
Drafted second BRR Phase 1 policy task (Task Safety Classification) and refreshed worker/advisor handoff artifacts.

Reviewed the new Task Safety Classification against the task criteria and the existing Owner Review Matrix. The class model is concrete, directly mapped, docs-only, and internally coherent. The worker's flagged design choice — separating execution from acceptance — is the right framing here because it cleanly distinguishes Class B from Class A and Class C without prematurely implementing Phase 2 flow. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
Drafted third BRR Phase 1 policy task (Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy) and refreshed worker/advisor handoff artifacts.

Reviewed the new Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy against the task criteria and the prior BRR policy sections. All seven required triggers are covered, verdict reuse is explicit, and the policy stays aligned with the Owner Review Matrix and Task Safety Classification without inventing new enforcement behavior. The two looser mappings called out by the worker are acceptable as written: they disclose that those triggers are related by purpose and pattern rather than pretending to be exact row lookups. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
Drafted fourth BRR Phase 1 policy task (Operating Definitions) and refreshed worker/advisor handoff artifacts.

Reviewed the new Operating Definitions against the task criteria and the three prior BRR policy sections. The six required terms are now explicit, cross-reconciled, and consistent with the existing matrix, classification, and stop-policy language. The worker was also right not to declare the phase transition unilaterally: BRR Phase 1 policy scope is now complete, but deciding whether to continue hardening Phase 1, move to Phase 2, or choose another lane is a separate boundary decision. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
